key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation model with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration state prediction across very different welding processes, including TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for costly relabeling when deploying intelligent weld monitoring to new processes, improving practical usability across heterogeneous welding systems.
method: Train a UDA classifier to learn domain-invariant penetration features from labeled source data and unlabeled target data.
Use gradual source domain expansion to progressively incorporate more diverse source samples and stabilize transfer.
Validate both same-process and cross-process transfer between TIG and laser welding datasets.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average accuracies of 90.65% on TIGFH and 90.72% on LSPS in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser plus 81.13% laser-to-TIG in cross-process transfer, all far above the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the similarity and quality of available source/target welding data, so transfer results should be revalidated when process settings, sensors, or joint conditions change.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
